rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF YUK SHO WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/9/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was just e-filed on 4/6/26, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) A Corrected I&A was filed on 3/30/26 but no proof of service of notice of how to object to it has been filed. (4) The cash on hand in the Corrected I&A is now reported to be $29,192.16 as of 4/5/24, but the statement showed $26,908.16 as of 3/28/25. No reconciliation is shown. The amended accounting petition has not been fully reviewed yet, as no courtesy copy was received timely. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies to the assigned examiner by 5/26/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The court investigator and the probate examiner are NOT interested parties, to whom notice is required to be served.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINCOLN CHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine whether this petition was timely filed under Probate Code section 8226(c)(2). Did petitioner "first obtain knowledge of the will" within the meaning of section 8226(c)(2) on 12/19//2025 when he received a copy of the will from the decedent's estate planning attorney Jeffrey Tsu? If so, the 60-day filing deadline under section 8226(c)(2) expired on 02/17/2026. Accoring to his supplement, petitioner received the original from Jeffrey Tsu on or about 02/02/2026. Petitioner did not deposit the original will with the clerk until 02/19/2026 (in PDW-26-063475) and further delayed filing this petition for probate of the will until 03/09/2026. Petitioner could have petitioned for probate of the will as a lost will as soon as petitioner received a copy of the will on December 19, 2025, but elected not to do so. Petitioner argues in his supplement that section 8226(c)(2) only applies when a different will was initially admitted to probate (and not when a determination of intestacy was initially made), but see Estate of Earley (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 369, 377 ["In sum, section 8226 provides filing deadlines for admitting a will to probate regardless of whether another will has already been admitted to probate or whether estate proceedings have already commenced based on a determination of intestacy."] PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JACK M. WONG RESUDIARY TRUST, DATED DECEMBER 15, 1996 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/30/2026 per stipulation of counsel (via e-mail on 04/07/2026). A preliminary review indicates the following issues: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice in a contested matter. Proof of service must be filed separately. LRSF 14.7(A)(4). 2) No declaration re retention and compliance with inspection of original financial account statements required by CRC 2.257 and LRSF 14.59(A) is on file. 3) Direct notice must be given to each beneficiary, absent a waiver signed by each beneficiary's counsel of record (Probate Code section 1204) or a directive signed by each beneficary that notice to his/her counsel is sufficient (CRC 7.51(a)(2)). 4) Albert Wong's counsel was served by eletronic means, but no consent to eletrconic service (EFS-005-CV form) is on file. Section 1215(c)(1).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DORIS JOHANNA MEHLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues remain: 1) Petition is incomplete and inconsistent: In Item 5a, box 3 or 4 must be checked, and box 7 or 8 must be checked. Item 7 is blank, although it appears to apply. If a will names a trust or trustee as a devisee, the trustee must be clearly identified in item 8 . Where the personal representative and the sole trustee are the same person, or if there is no trustee, the beneficiaries of the trust must be clearly identified (Probate Code Section 1208(b) and San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.C.2). 2) The instant petition indicates that the named executors have declined to serve, but no declinations have been filed. 3) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 4) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States (Probate Code Section 8113). 5) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 6) No proof of publication has been filed (Probate Code Sections 8120 and 8121). 7) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE EMILIA WELBORN 2007 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: For Remedy For Breach Of Trust By Trustee (2) To Suspend And Remove Trustee; (3) For Appointment Of A Professional Fiduciary To Serve As Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/2/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The amended petition was filed on 3/16/26 and not e-filed until 3/17/26, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. Trust petitions require 30 days' notice. (3) The proof of service filed on 3/17/26 does not reflect the correct, complete title of the amended petition, and item 3 of the notice of hearing was not completed. (4) A copy of the amended petition was apparently not served with the notice. (5) A petition pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 must be personally served on any any heir who would be entitled to inherit if the trust were not valid. (6) Notice was not given to Gordon Gibbs, who is reportedly the executor of Angela Hall's will. (7) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (8) The petition does not propose who the private professional fiduciary successor trustee would be. Consent by the proposed successor trustee must be filed. The petition has not been fully reviewed, as no courtesy copy was received. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Please provide a courtesy copy of the petition, a proposed order, and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/22/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW LIVI MITCHELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/26/2026. The petition will not be thoroughly reviewed until the following documents are filed with the court: 1) Duties and Liabilities (Form DE-147) (Probate Code Section 8404). 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S)(San Francisco Local Rule 14.18E). 3) Proof of publication (Probate Code Sections 8120 and 8121). 4) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (Form DE-121) (Probate Code Section 8100). All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 5) A proposed order must be submitted or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies to this examiner no later than 05/12/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL ROBERT BOLAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 10/19/2027.
